channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Relativity Space Plans Private Mars Orbiter with NASA Scientific Instruments for 2028 Launch
item.description: Relativity Space, a U.S. aerospace company best known for developing the Terran R launch vehicle scheduled for debut around 2027, has announced an unexpected plan to send a Mars orbiter in 2028. The c...

item.title: USA takes a 2-0 lead over Australia in FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match
item.description: The FIFA World Cup 2026 Group D match between the United States and Australia at Lumen Field in Seattle saw the hosts establish a comfortable 2-0 advantage. The United States entered the fixture after...

item.title: India–UK CETA to boost wider ties beyond trade, says Foreign Secretary
item.description: Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri has stated that the India–UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA), which is set to come into force on July 15, represents much more than a conventional trad...

item.title: Senate Scrutiny Grows Over Todd Blanche's Refusal to Formally End Controversial Federal Fund
item.description: Acting U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche is facing increased political and legal pressure after declining to provide a federal court with a written commitment that the Trump administration's controve...

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Photos Reveal Kathleen Turner’s Breakthrough Experience Filming Body Heat (1981)
item.description: The article showcases 25 behind-the-scenes photographs of actress Kathleen Turner during the production of the 1981 neo-noir film Body Heat, the movie that launched her career as a major Hollywood sta...

item.title: Cape Town launches winter holiday programme for youth across libraries and recreation centres
item.description: Cape Town’s City authorities have announced the launch of a winter holiday programme aimed at keeping young people engaged and active during the school break, starting on 26 June 2026. The initiative ...

item.title: Russia May Permanently Seal the Leaking Zvezda Transfer Compartment on the ISS to Reduce Risk
item.description: The article examines a possible response by Roscosmos to persistent air leaks originating from the Transfer Chamber (PrK) of the Russian Zvezda module aboard the International Space Station (ISS). Ear...

item.title: 1964 Experimental Portrait Session of Gena Rowlands Reimagining Classic Paintings by Leo Fuchs
item.description: This article explores a creative and experimental 1964 photo session featuring actress Gena Rowlands and photographer Leo Fuchs. The project, known as the “Master Painters” series, aimed to reinterpre...

item.title: White House responds to reporter criticism of Iran deal with personal attack
item.description: A White House rapid-response account sparked controversy after issuing a personal insult toward journalist Batya Ungar-Sargon, who criticized President Donald Trump's preliminary agreement with Iran. ...

item.title: Senona rejects allegations of sidelining colleague and defends actions before Madlanga inquiry
item.description: Suspended KwaZulu-Natal Hawks head Maj-Gen Lesetja Senona appeared before the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry on 19 June 2026 to give evidence relating to the 2021 theft of 541kg of confiscated cocaine...

item.title: Report: White House Staff Monitored Trump’s Trash After He Discarded Valuable Silverware
item.description: According to a report based on an upcoming book by New York Times journalists Maggie Haberman and Jonathan Swan, White House staff during President Donald Trump’s second term began monitoring his tras...

item.title: Report alleges DOJ has withheld Epstein email records that may reference Trump connections
item.description: A recent report cited by CBS News and summarized in Raw Story claims that the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) may still be withholding a substantial number of documents related to convicted sex offen...

item.title: Israeli airstrikes kill 18 in southern Lebanon after four Israeli soldiers are killed amid renewed clashes with Hezbollah
item.description: At least 18 people were killed and 33 wounded in a series of Israeli airstrikes across southern Lebanon as fighting between Israel and Hezbollah intensified despite efforts to reduce regional tensions...

item.title: George Will argues Trump presidency is weakening after controversial Iran agreement
item.description: A conservative columnist argues that President Donald Trump’s political influence and leadership are entering a period of decline following a controversial agreement to end U.S. military involvement i...

item.title: Opinion piece questions Tim Wilson's conduct following heated Today show interview on Labor tax reforms
item.description: This opinion article by Rachel Withers examines the recent public behaviour of Australian shadow treasurer Tim Wilson, focusing on a contentious appearance on the Today show. During the interview, Wil...

item.title: Report: Last-Minute Chaos Surrounds Signing of U.S.-Iran Agreement at Versailles Dinner
item.description: According to a report by Agence France-Presse cited in the article, the signing of a major U.S.-Iran agreement was marked by unexpected logistical confusion and shifting plans. The agreement had been ...

item.title: Acting Intelligence Director Bill Pulte Reportedly Considers Large-Scale Staff Changes Amid Debate Over Election Security Assessments
item.description: According to reports cited by Raw Story, Bill Pulte assumed the role of acting Director of National Intelligence without Senate confirmation and may serve in the position for up to 210 days under the ...

item.title: Analysis of JD Vance's New Book Suggests He Is Positioning Himself Beyond the Trump Era
item.description: A Raw Story article highlights an analysis by author Colin Dickey, published in The New Republic, concerning Vice President JD Vance's new book, 'Communion.' According to Dickey, the book provides ins...

item.title: U.S. Stocks Rebound as Semiconductor Shares Lead Market Recovery Following Fed-Induced Sell-Off
item.description: U.S. stock markets finished higher on Thursday, recovering from losses triggered the previous day by concerns over the Federal Reserve's policy outlook. The S&P 500 gained 1.08% to close at 7,500.58, ...

item.title: Amazon MGM Drops Release Plans for Luca Guadagnino’s OpenAI-Themed Film ‘Artificial’
item.description: Amazon MGM Studios has decided not to release 'Artificial,' an upcoming film directed by Luca Guadagnino that focuses on the 2023 leadership crisis at OpenAI. The movie, which stars Andrew Garfield as...
